sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Angela, presented with a history of recurrent kidney stones, with this being her seventh or eighth episode.
She reported that the current stone had passed approximately three days ago and she is no longer experiencing pain or blood in her urine.
She also has a past medical history of hypertension and diabetes.
Angela's blood pressure was 150/90 at the visit, but she reported that her home blood pressure readings are typically 130-140/60-70.
She is taking 2.5 mg of norvasc daily for her hypertension and 500 mg of metformin daily for her diabetes.
She has a history of eating a diet high in fast food and sugary snacks, which she is trying to change.
She has not been checking her blood sugars frequently, but reports they have been "pretty good".
She has a history of insomnia and has tried meditation apps to help with sleep.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, diabetes, and recurrent kidney stones.
She is taking 2.5 mg of norvasc daily for her hypertension and 500 mg of metformin daily for her diabetes.
She has a history of eating a diet high in fast food and sugary snacks.
Review of systems: No chest pain, belly pain, or other symptoms were reported.
Current medications:
Norvasc 2.5 mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500 mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 150/90, but patient reports home readings of 130-140/60-70.
Physical exam: Lungs were clear bilaterally, heart exam revealed a grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, and abdominal exam showed no tenderness to palpation.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a history of recurrent kidney stones, hypertension, and diabetes, but currently appears to be doing well with her current treatment plan.

Problem list: Kidney stones, hypertension, diabetes; status: active; suspected recurrent kidney stones; status: active; past medical history of hypertension and diabetes; status: past; suspected need for nutrition consult; status: suspected.
Plan:
I prescribed Percocet as needed for pain, but it is unclear if this was a prescription from the emergency room visit or a new prescription.
I also prescribed 10 mg of melatonin as needed for insomnia.
I will refer the patient to urology to investigate the cause of her recurrent kidney stones.
I will also refer the patient to nutrition to help her change her diet and manage her hypertension and diabetes.
I will schedule a follow-up appointment in three months to check on her progress.
Follow-up:
The patient will return in three months for a follow-up appointment.
